title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## Linked employer-employee data: March 2024 year
Annual linked employer-employee data (LEED) provides person-level statistics for the March year about New Zealanders’ interaction with the labour market and their sources of income.

## LEED annual technical notes – 2025 update
Technical notes about the annual statistics produced from the Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED).

## Employment indicators: October 2025
Employment indicators provide an early indication of changes in the labour market.

## Linked employer-employee data: September 2024 quarter
Quarterly linked employer-employee data (LEED) provides statistics on filled jobs, job flows, worker flows, mean and median earnings for continuing jobs and new hires, and total earnings.

## Unemployment rate at 5.3 percent in the September 2025 quarter
New Zealand’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate was 5.3 percent in the September 2025 quarter.

## Labour market statistics: September 2025 quarter
Labour market statistics provide a picture of the New Zealand labour market, including unemployment and employment rates, demand for labour, and changes in wages and salaries.

## Employment indicators: September 2025
Employment indicators provide an early indication of changes in the labour market.

## Household labour force survey estimated working-age population: September 2025 quarter
The household labour force survey estimated working-age population table shows the population benchmarks used to produce household labour force survey estimates for the upcoming labour market statistics release.

## Employment indicators: August 2025
Employment indicators provide an early indication of changes in the labour market.

## Economic snapshot: June 2025 quarter
Our economic snapshot summarises important economic statistics for the June 2025 quarter.

## International trade: June 2025 quarter – data sources and methods
Read about updates to the data sources used to compile the international trade release.

## Employment indicators: July 2025
Employment indicators provide an early indication of changes in the labour market.
